phase_name: Fix: Homebrew PATH not inherited by app bundle subprocesses
goal: When YapTap.app is launched from Finder, the macOS process environment PATH is minimal (/usr/bin:/bin:/usr/sbin:/sbin) and does not include Homebrew's binary directories (/opt/homebrew/bin on Apple Silicon, /usr/local/bin on Intel). This causes the ffmpeg and python3 pre-flight checks — and all subprocess spawns — to fail even when those tools are installed. The fix is a single `brew_augmented_path()` helper in config.rs that prepends known Homebrew/common locations to the inherited PATH, applied uniformly to every Command spawn in the app.
root_cause: macOS does not source the user's shell profile (.zshrc, .bash_profile) for GUI apps launched via Finder, Dock, or login items. Homebrew modifies the shell PATH but this is invisible to the app bundle process.

tasks.title: Add `pub fn brew_augmented_path() -> String` to `src/config.rs`
checks: Run `cargo build` exit 0; `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.
On Apple Silicon: `brew_augmented_path()` contains `/opt/homebrew/bin` as the first segment.
On Intel Mac: `brew_augmented_path()` contains `/usr/local/bin` as the first segment.
The existing PATH segments are preserved at the end.

tasks.title: Apply `brew_augmented_path()` to pre-flight checks in `run_pipeline_inner()` (`src/app.rs`)
tasks.description:
The two pre-flight `Command` calls in `run_pipeline_inner()` use bare executable names that fail when launched from Finder. Add `.env("PATH", crate::config::brew_augmented_path())` to both.

Current python3 check:
```rust
let py_ok = std::process::Command::new("python3")
    .arg("--version")
    .output()
    .map(|o| o.status.success())
    .unwrap_or(false);
```
Add `.env("PATH", crate::config::brew_augmented_path())` before `.output()`.

Current ffmpeg check:
```rust
let ff_ok = std::process::Command::new("ffmpeg")
    .arg("-version")
    .output()
    .map(|o| o.status.success())
    .unwrap_or(false);
```
Add `.env("PATH", crate::config::brew_augmented_path())` before `.output()`.
checks: Run `cargo build` exit 0; `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.
Launch `dist/YapTap.app` from Finder with ffmpeg installed in /opt/homebrew/bin — pipeline does not fail with 'ffmpeg not found on PATH'.
Remove ffmpeg temporarily: pipeline fails with 'ffmpeg not found on PATH' (check still works).

tasks.title: Apply `brew_augmented_path()` to `run_setup_commands()` in `src/app.rs`
tasks.description:
The `python3 -m venv` command in `run_setup_commands()` will fail on Finder-launched app if python3 is in /opt/homebrew/bin. Add `.env("PATH", crate::config::brew_augmented_path())` to the `Command::new("python3")` call.

The `pip` call already uses a full path from the venv, so no change needed there.
checks: Run `cargo build` exit 0; `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.
Remove `~/.config/yaptap/.venv/`; launch app from Finder — first-launch setup completes without 'failed to spawn python3' error.

tasks.title: Apply `brew_augmented_path()` to the subprocess `Command` in `run_transcription()` (`src/transcription.rs`)
tasks.description:
The Python subprocess spawned for transcription runs `whisper`, which calls `ffmpeg` internally. Without an augmented PATH, whisper cannot locate ffmpeg even if it is installed. Add `.env("PATH", crate::config::brew_augmented_path())` to the `Command` builder in `run_transcription()`.

Current:
```rust
let mut cmd = Command::new(crate::config::python_interpreter());
cmd.arg(&script)
    .arg(wav_path)
    .args(["--model", model])
    .stdout(Stdio::piped())
    .stderr(Stdio::piped());
```

Add after `.stderr(Stdio::piped())`:
```rust
    .env("PATH", crate::config::brew_augmented_path())
```
checks: Run `cargo build` exit 0; `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.
Launch from Finder; press hotkey, speak, stop — transcription completes without 'No such file or directory: ffmpeg' from whisper.

tasks.title: Apply `brew_augmented_path()` to the subprocess `Command` in `run_llm_collect()` (`src/llm.rs`)
tasks.description:
Mirror P9-T004 in `run_llm_collect()`. The LLM subprocess also inherits the app's minimal PATH; while Ollama is usually at a fixed path, consistency requires augmenting PATH here too.

Add `.env("PATH", crate::config::brew_augmented_path())` to the `Command` builder in `run_llm_collect()`, after `.stderr(Stdio::piped())`.
checks: Run `cargo build` exit 0; `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.
Launch from Finder with a prompt selected; trigger the pipeline — LLM step completes successfully.

tasks.title: Fix the post-setup `which ffmpeg` check in `run_first_launch_setup()` (`src/app.rs`)
tasks.description:
The `Command::new("which").arg("ffmpeg")` call in `run_first_launch_setup()` also runs in the minimal PATH context and will incorrectly report ffmpeg as missing even when it is installed.

Replace:
```rust
let ffmpeg_ok = std::process::Command::new("which")
    .arg("ffmpeg")
    .output()
    .map(|o| o.status.success())
    .unwrap_or(false);
```

With:
```rust
let ffmpeg_ok = std::process::Command::new("ffmpeg")
    .arg("-version")
    .env("PATH", crate::config::brew_augmented_path())
    .output()
    .map(|o| o.status.success())
    .unwrap_or(false);
```

Using `ffmpeg -version` with the augmented PATH is both more direct and consistent with the pre-flight check in `run_pipeline_inner()`.
checks: Run `cargo build` exit 0; `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` clean.
Remove venv; launch from Finder — first-launch setup completes and the ffmpeg alert does NOT appear when ffmpeg is installed in /opt/homebrew/bin.
With ffmpeg uninstalled: ffmpeg alert appears after setup.

tasks.title: Verify `cargo build --release`, `cargo clippy -- -D warnings`, and `cargo test` pass clean
tasks.description: After all P9 changes, run a full build, lint, and test pass.
checks: Run `cargo build --release` — exit 0.
Run `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` — no warnings, exit 0.
Run `cargo test` — all tests pass.

tasks.title: End-to-end smoke test: launch from Finder with Homebrew ffmpeg and python3
tasks.description: Verify the full pipeline works when the app is launched from Finder (no shell PATH).
checks: Run `make app`.
Launch `dist/YapTap.app` from Finder (not terminal).
Press hotkey, speak for 3 seconds, release — transcription completes and raw transcript appears on clipboard. No 'ffmpeg not found' or 'python3 not found' alert.
Select a prompt; repeat — LLM result appears on clipboard. No 'Ollama not running' alert (assuming Ollama is running).
Quit and relaunch from Finder — no first-launch setup alert (venv already present).
